topic: Sharp partial identification and Neyman-orthogonal root-n inference for a NON-SEPARABLE policy-relevant functional of the joint gain law Y(1)-Y(0) (focal: the two-sided gain-band psi=E_omega[1{a<=Y(1)-Y(0)<=b}], the policy-affected share whose gain falls in a band, and the induced gain-dispersion index) in the generalized Roy/MTE model of arXiv 2604.12263 with a discrete IV. The anchor's 1-D closed-form quantile-coupling reduction and DML both rest on linearity-in-y; for the non-supermodular band cost separability is lost, so the sharp identified set is the value of a rank-structured constrained OT / linear-fractional LP (Charnes-Cooper) with an analyst-specified copula-sensitivity restriction R(eps) on the finite joint-outcome grid, and inference is rebuilt on the LP-dual optimizer as the Neyman-orthogonal influence function (root-n + asymptotic normality under dual uniqueness; Fang-Santos directional bootstrap otherwise). Extends Rem general_functional / Rem marx-open.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive sharp lower and upper bounds for the two-sided gain-band probability E_omega[1{a <= Y(1)-Y(0) <= b}] in a discrete-IV generalized Roy/MTE model when the finite-grid joint law obeys rank restrictions and a copula-sensitivity restriction R(epsilon).

source_refs.claim: The generalized-Roy CCOT problem reduces to separable one-dimensional OT only for product costs, and its discrete-IV DML result is for those bounds.
source_refs.author: Jiyuan Tan, Jose Blanchet, Vasilis Syrgkanis

source_refs.claim: Sharp latent-index/MTE bounds use identified marginals and stochastic orders for linear functionals, not a copula-restricted joint gain event.
source_refs.author: Philip Marx

source_refs.claim: The paper studies partial identification of supermodular and indicator functionals of the joint potential-outcome law after conditional marginals are identified.
source_refs.author: Yanqin Fan, Emmanuel Guerre, Dongming Zhu

source_refs.excerpt: recast the paper around the factual-compatibility theorem with a computable response-type feasibility correction
each covariate-dose-IV cell need not be pasteable into one full-data law
missing interval-band/measurable-selector attainment for the exact sharp GPN interval

open_problems.why_unsolved: The anchor's closed form depends on a product-cost, one-dimensional reduction, but the two-sided band is a nonseparable joint-gain event and R(epsilon) couples the cells that reduction treats separately. The failed OT-DTE work supplies a concrete additional obstruction: cellwise feasible tables need not paste into one full-data response-type law. Hence an outer constrained-OT/Charnes-Cooper program is not yet a sharp identified set until its feasible points are shown equivalent to admissible common Roy response-type laws and its endpoints are attained.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A necessary-and-sufficient sharpness theorem equating the endpoints to a finite rank-structured constrained-OT or Charnes-Cooper LP, with a response-type feasibility characterization and a constructive common coupling attaining each endpoint under R(epsilon).

writing_exemplar.why: It is the direct generalized-Roy PRTE motivation and exposes the product-cost boundary that the gain-band target crosses.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: limited IV support leaves policy-relevant effects unidentified -> generalized Roy identifies restricted marginal policy information -> a nonseparable gain-band needs a sharp common-coupling characterization beyond product-cost CCOT

method_exemplars.technique: Adapt the extremal-coupling/Fréchet argument for an indicator functional and prove endpoint attainability rather than only an outer bound.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The target is an indicator of the unobserved joint potential-outcome law; the new constraints are the Roy rank and finite-grid copula restrictions.

method_exemplars.technique: Use OT duality to express sharp joint-potential-outcome endpoints as sample-dual values and establish root-n stability from the dual representation.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Once sharpness is proved, the proposed endpoint is likewise a joint-potential-outcome OT value, with additional Roy/IV feasibility restrictions.

open_problems.open_problem: Establish valid root-n inference for sharp nonseparable gain-band LP endpoints with machine-learned nuisances: specify when a unique constrained-LP dual yields a Neyman-orthogonal influence function and when a directionally valid bootstrap is required at dual ties.

source_refs.claim: For discrete instruments the paper develops Neyman-orthogonal DML scores with root-n asymptotic normality for its separable product-cost bounds.
source_refs.author: Jiyuan Tan, Jose Blanchet, Vasilis Syrgkanis

source_refs.claim: Sharp joint-potential-outcome functional bounds are represented through OT duals with root-n estimation and bootstrap inference under regularity conditions.
source_refs.author: Daniel Ober-Reynolds

source_refs.claim: Ordinary bootstrap consistency with a Gaussian first stage requires full differentiability; the paper gives an alternative for directionally differentiable maps.
source_refs.author: Zheng Fang, Andres Santos

source_refs.excerpt: The estimator/inference layer required new off-piece score and remainder control
Active-face uniqueness and first-order dual-gap separation are imposed as subclass restrictions, not derived

open_problems.why_unsolved: A nonseparable endpoint is a constrained-LP value whose active face can change with estimated Roy/MTE moments. The anchor's orthogonal score is obtained after a product-cost reduction, while the prior copula-band attempt could not discharge its off-piece score and remainder control. Existing PRTE-OT work explicitly assumes, rather than derives, unique active faces and separated dual gaps; at a tie, the value map is only directionally differentiable, exactly the regime in which Fang--Santos rules out the ordinary bootstrap.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A cross-fitted orthogonal-score expansion with the unique constrained-LP dual as influence function under stated primitive nondegeneracy and nuisance product-rate conditions, plus a Hadamard-directional derivative and Fang--Santos bootstrap-validity theorem for nonunique-dual regimes.

writing_exemplar.why: Its transition from sharp PRTE identification to discrete-IV orthogonal DML is the motivation arc the nonseparable endpoint extends.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: limited IV support creates a sharp partial-identification problem -> OT turns a bound into an estimable value -> nonseparable constrained-OT endpoints require regular dual-based inference or directional inference at kinks

method_exemplars.technique: Use OT duality to obtain a sample-dual endpoint estimator and a root-n expansion from stability of its dual solution.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Both targets are sharp OT values for joint-potential-outcome functionals; the proposed problem adds IV rank constraints and cross-fitted nuisance estimation.

method_exemplars.technique: Derive the directional derivative of the LP value map and apply derivative-estimated modified resampling when the usual bootstrap fails.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Dual ties and active-basis changes make the gain-band endpoint a directionally but not fully differentiable optimized functional.

method_exemplars.technique: Port the discrete-IV generalized-Roy nuisance decomposition and Neyman-orthogonal score construction before replacing the separable endpoint by the constrained-LP dual.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies the same observed IV/MTE primitives that populate the proposed LP's estimated right-hand side.

open_problems.open_problem: Give an observable finite-discrete-IV iff frontier for when a policy-relevant MTE weight is supported by observed instrument margins, versus when a nonzero extrapolation width remains after the model's shape restrictions.

source_refs.excerpt: Observed-face quotient support and policy-mixture equivalence (NOT YET PROVED)
a strict new bound, open-regime theorem, or iff frontier between named estimators

open_problems.why_unsolved: The rejected multi-IV proposal identifies the exact missing object: a zero-width LP face is not automatically equivalent to the policy weight being a mixture of observed IV margins once flat directions induced by shape constraints are quotiented out. Its reviewer demanded an open-regime or iff frontier, so routine finite-LP duality does not settle the observable distinction between genuine policy support and residual extrapolation. This is directly relevant to which discrete-IV gain-band targets could ever collapse to point identification before their OT coupling ambiguity is considered.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-dimensional convex-geometry iff theorem equating zero policy extrapolation width to a quotient-space mixture/support condition, with an open-set separation from responsive-group LATE identification.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames PRTEs as reweightings of latent-selection information and makes the limited-support obstacle concrete.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: discrete instruments reveal selected latent-resistance margins -> policy effects reweight those margins -> an observable support frontier is needed to decide whether the policy target is identified or extrapolative

method_exemplars.technique: Represent the PRTE restriction and target as a constrained transport/linear program and use primal-dual geometry to characterize endpoint width.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired frontier distinguishes a point-identified policy functional from a residual-width discrete-IV LP under the same generalized-Roy primitives.

method_exemplars.technique: Use cross-fitting and orthogonal endpoint scores to carry nuisance uncertainty through a sharp sensitivity-bound optimization.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The frontier produces either a point target or sharp endpoints whose inference remains driven by learned IV/MTE nuisance components.

open_problems.open_problem: Characterize the sharp jointly feasible copula-sensitivity frontier epsilon -> [psi_L(epsilon), psi_U(epsilon)] for a finite collection of gain bands and an induced gain-dispersion index, rather than applying separately optimized coupling bounds at each band or sensitivity radius.

source_refs.claim: Makarov bounds can be sharp pointwise but not uniformly sharp, so functionals assembled from pointwise bounds need not be sharp.
source_refs.author: Sergio Firpo, Geert Ridder

source_refs.claim: Integrating conditional pointwise-sharp treatment-effect bounds can be conservative because the pointwise bounds are not uniformly sharp; sharp bounds search over an admissible copula set.
source_refs.author: Brigham R. Frandsen, Lars J. Lefgren

source_refs.claim: Generalized-Roy PRTE partial identification is represented as CCOT, but the analytic solution is for the product-cost separable subclass.
source_refs.author: Jiyuan Tan, Jose Blanchet, Vasilis Syrgkanis

open_problems.why_unsolved: Every band threshold and every epsilon must be generated by the same latent coupling. Firpo--Ridder and Frandsen--Lefgren identify the concrete incompatibility: separately pointwise-sharp extrema need not belong to any common treatment-effect distribution. The anchor's separable CCOT theorem does not enforce cross-band or cross-epsilon compatibility for a non-product cost, so a displayed collection of scalar endpoints is not yet an interpretable sensitivity analysis of one joint gain law.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A theorem identifying the complete finite-grid band vector and epsilon-indexed sharp set as the projection or value function of one common rank-structured constrained-OT polytope, including a feasibility characterization and an attaining coupling for every reported frontier point.

writing_exemplar.why: Its framing makes the pointwise-versus-uniform-sharpness failure concrete before introducing a structured copula class.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: potential-outcome marginals do not identify a treatment-effect distribution -> pointwise sharp bounds cannot generally be assembled into one distribution -> construct a jointly feasible copula-restricted gain-band sensitivity frontier

method_exemplars.technique: Optimize directly over one admissible copula class and prove sharpness with a single feasible joint distribution rather than separate threshold-wise extremizers.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The proposed frontier likewise requires one coupling to satisfy all band, rank, and sensitivity restrictions simultaneously.

method_exemplars.technique: Estimate OT-dual sharp endpoints through sample analogues and use the dual representation for root-n uncertainty quantification.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Each coordinate of the common-coupling frontier is a sharp joint-potential-outcome OT value, but the common polytope preserves their joint feasibility.

literature_map:
Tan, Blanchet, and Syrgkanis (2026, arXiv:2604.12263) formulate generalized-Roy PRTE partial identification as constrained conditional OT over potential outcomes and latent resistance.
Their analytic simplification reduces the multidimensional problem to separable one-dimensional OT with product costs; this is the direct boundary for a nonseparable gain-band cost.
Their discrete-IV result supplies Neyman-orthogonal DML with root-n asymptotic normality, but only after that separable reduction.
Marx (2024, Journal of Econometrics, 10.1016/j.jeconom.2023.105561) gives sharp latent-index/MTE bounds for linear functionals using stochastic orders and identified marginals.
Fan, Guerre, and Zhu (2017, Journal of Econometrics, 10.1016/j.jeconom.2016.10.005) study supermodular and indicator functionals of the joint potential-outcome law once marginals are identified.
Firpo and Ridder (2019, Journal of Econometrics, 10.1016/j.jeconom.2019.04.012) establish that Makarov bounds can be pointwise but not uniformly sharp for distributional treatment-effect functionals.
Frandsen and Lefgren (2021, Quantitative Economics, 10.3982/QE1273) make the common-coupling issue operational through structured copula restrictions.
Ober-Reynolds (2023, arXiv:2311.09435) represents sharp joint-potential-outcome functional bounds by OT values and develops dual-based root-n estimation.
Gao, Ge, and Qian (2025, AISTATS; arXiv:2406.07868) provide a multi-marginal-OT representation for causal partial identification.
Fan, Pass, and Shi (2025, arXiv:2503.16098) characterize incomplete-data identified sets using OT cost inequalities and support functions.
Lin, Gao, Blanchet, and Glynn (2026, arXiv:2602.20681) obtain COT inference under smooth-density/quadratic-cost structure, showing how special existing normality conditions are.
Fang and Santos (2019, ReStud, 10.1093/restud/rdy049) give the nonregular-inference boundary: the ordinary bootstrap generally fails for merely directionally differentiable maps.
The live gap is sharp nonseparable, rank/coplanar constrained-OT gain functionals with a common-coupling proof and regular-or-directional DML inference; none of these papers supplies that combination.
prior_proposal_map:
The current qid directory contains only state/pipeline material, not an earlier proposal draft.
Failed pid_ot_dte_marginal/v1 was mined: its rejected OT/DTE angles record that locally feasible tables need not be pasteable into a full-data law and recommend a factual-compatibility/response-type correction.
Its reviewer trail reinforces that a pastability lemma and fully displayed finite-LP certificate were missing.
Failed pid_poc_tau_band/concordance_sharp_dr was mined: its README records unresolved interval-band attainment and off-piece score/remainder control for copula-sensitivity endpoints.
Failed stat_prte_ot_boundary_adaptation/v1 reviews were mined: active-face uniqueness and first-order dual-gap separation were imposed as subclass restrictions rather than derived.
Downgraded pid_mte_multi_iv/v1 rejected angle1 and its reviewer JSON were mined: the observed-face quotient-support/policy-mixture iff is explicitly not proved, and the reviewer asks for an iff frontier or open-regime theorem.
No upgrade parent qid/spec was supplied.
